_comment: kern plugin registry seed. 2 official (kern/sample) + 8 community plugins. Shape mirrors the D1 catalog (§6.3). Swap lib/registry.ts to a live Worker fetch when the backend exists.

plugins.description: Run and manage Minecraft Java Edition servers. Auto-downloads the correct JAR/installer, accepts EULA, edits server.properties, manages whitelist/ops/bans, and backs up worlds.
plugins.author: kern/sample

versions.changelog: Adds quilt + neoforge softwares, world backup/restore.
Initial release. vanilla, paper, purpur, fabric, forge.

plugins.readme_md:
# minecraft java server

the flagship first-party plugin. teaches kern how to run minecraft java edition servers across **7 server softwares**: vanilla, paper, purpur, fabric, forge, neoforge, quilt.

## what it does

- **auto-downloads** the correct jar or installer for the chosen software + version.
- accepts the **eula** (scaffolded into `eula.txt`).
- edits `server.properties` from the config form.
- manages **whitelist / ops / bans**.
- **backs up and restores worlds**.
- registers two tabs in the server detail view: **setup** and **chat**.

## config

the host renders the config form from `configSchema`. pick a runtime, set the minecraft version, tune jvm args. defaults are sane — paper 1.21, 2g heap.

## lifecycle

`start` runs `java -jar server.jar --nogui`, with runtime-qualified overrides (e.g. `start.forge` runs the forge installer with `useShell`).

> **note** `stop` sends `stop` to stdin and waits up to 15s for a clean world save before hard-kill.

plugins.description: Scaffold and run Discord bots across runtimes. Runtime-conditional scaffolding generates the right starter files for each language.
plugins.author: kern/sample
plugins.readme_md:
# discord bot manager

a first-party plugin for running discord bots. supports **4 runtimes**: node.js, bun, deno, rust.

## runtime-conditional scaffolding

the plugin generates the right starter files for the chosen runtime — a `package.json` for node/bun/deno and a `cargo.toml` for rust.

## lifecycle

runtime-qualified keys override the base start command:

- `start.node` → `node index.js`
- `start.bun` → `bun index.js`
- `start.deno` → `deno run --allow-net index.js`
- `start.rust` → runs the compiled binary via `useShell`

## config

set the runtime, the entry file, and your bot token (kept in userOverrides, never shipped in the manifest).

plugins.description: Run a dedicated Terraria server via tShock. Auto-fetches the build, manages config and world files.
plugins.author: redstonewolf

versions.changelog: World autosave hook on graceful stop.

plugins.readme_md:
# terraria server (tshock)

run a dedicated terraria server via tShock. the plugin fetches the build, manages `serverconfig.txt`, and stops cleanly so the world saves.

## config

set the tshock build, max players, and world name. the plugin scaffolds a fresh world on first start if none exists.

plugins.description: Run a headless Factorio server. Manages saves, mods, and autosave slots with graceful lifecycle.
plugins.author: smeltingpro
plugins.readme_md:
# factorio headless

run a headless factorio server. the plugin manages save slots, mod lists, and ensures the world is flushed before teardown — factorio saves can be large, so the 15s graceful window matters.

plugins.description: Long-poll or webhook Telegram bots in Node/Bun. Handles token secrets and restart-on-crash.
plugins.author: nikolai_dev
plugins.readme_md:
# telegram bot

run telegram bots in node or bun. supports long-poll and webhook modes. the bot token is stored in userOverrides — never committed.

plugins.description: Serve a static site folder over nginx. Great for local dev previews and small self-hosted pages.
plugins.author: webworker42
plugins.readme_md:
# nginx static host

serve a static site folder over nginx. set the port and root dir. handy for local dev previews and small self-hosted pages without spinning up a full reverse-proxy config.

plugins.description: Spin up an ephemeral local PostgreSQL instance with a data dir, init scripts, and graceful checkpoint-on-stop.
plugins.author: datafox
plugins.readme_md:
# postgres local

spin up an ephemeral local postgresql instance. data lives in the instance dir, so it persists across restarts but is fully portable — copy the folder, copy the db.

## graceful stop

on `stop`, the plugin runs a checkpoint before teardown so no transactions are lost.

plugins.description: Run a local Redis instance with AOF persistence and config-driven maxmemory policies.
plugins.author: datafox
plugins.readme_md:
# redis local

run a local redis instance with aof persistence. set the port and maxmemory policy from the config form. pairs well with `postgres_local` for full local stacks.

plugins.description: Boot a Vite dev server for any front-end project. HMR over the live terminal, restart on config change.
plugins.author: webworker42
plugins.readme_md:
# vite dev server

boot a vite dev server for any front-end project. hmr output streams to the live terminal, and `restart` reloads the vite config without losing your editor tabs.

plugins.description: Self-host Grafana for local dashboards. Provisions datasources from the config form.
plugins.author: metrics_io
plugins.readme_md:
# grafana local

self-host grafana for local dashboards. the plugin provisions datasources from the config form so a fresh instance is ready to query on first start.
